headline: Youth Unemployment Persists at 11.4% Despite GDP Growth
context: DOSM reported that youth unemployment for 15-24 year olds remained stubbornly high at 11.4% in 2025, more than triple the national rate of 3.3%, with 480,000 young Malaysians unable to find work.

cards.big: 480,000 young Malaysians jobless at 11.4% youth unemployment — economy grows but they don't benefit
cards.sub: Graduate unemployment at 34% within first year suggests education system produces skills employers don't need

cards.big: Malaysia produces 280,000 graduates annually but economy creates only 185,000 graduate-level positions
cards.sub: 95,000 graduates yearly enter jobs below their qualification — pharmacists driving Grab, engineers selling insurance

cards.big: NEET rate among 15-24 year olds at 14.2% means 590,000 youth are not in education, employment, or training
cards.sub: Malay youth NEET rate at 16.8% versus Chinese at 9.2% — ethnic gap in youth economic participation widening

cards.big: Government spent RM8.4 billion on 72 separate youth employment programmes across 14 ministries in 2023-2025
cards.sub: No central tracking system — Human Resources Ministry cannot report how many programmes actually led to permanent jobs

cards.big: 72 youth employment programmes with no outcome tracking isn't a strategy — it's a RM8.4 billion guess
Should Malaysia merge all 72 youth employment programmes under one agency with mandatory job placement tracking?
